title: Connection<T...>​
content: </> interface Connection<T...> { Connected: boolean Disconnect: (Connection<T...>) → () Reconnect: (Connection<T...>) → () }

title: Disconnect​
content: </> Connection.Disconnect(self: Connection<T...>) → () Disconnects the connection from the signal. May be reconnected later using Connection:Reconnect(). local signal = LemonSignal.new() local connection = signal:Connect(print, "TEST1:") signal:Fire("Hello world!") -- TEST1: Hello world! connection:Disconnect() signal:Fire("Goodbye world!")

title: Reconnect​
content: </> Connection.Reconnect(self: Connection<T...>) → () Reconnects the connection to the signal again. May be disconnected later using Connection:Disconnect(). local signal = LemonSignal.new() local connection = signal:Connect(print, "TEST1:") signal:Fire("Hello world!") -- TEST1: Hello world! connection:Disconnect() signal:Fire("Goodbye world!") connection:Reconnect() signal:Fire("Hello again!") -- TEST1: Hello again!

title: Signal<U...>​
content: </> interface Signal<U...> { Connect: ( Signal<U...>, fn: (...any) → (), T... ) → Connection<T...> Once: ( Signal<U...>, fn: (...any) → (), T... ) → Connection<T...> Fire: ( Signal<U...>, U... ) → () DisconnectAll: (Signal<U...>) → () }

title: new​
content: </> Signal.new() → Signal Returns a new signal instance which can be used to connect functions. local LemonSignal = require(path.to.LemonSignal) local signal = LemonSignal.new()

title: Connect​
content: </> Signal.Connect( self: Signal<U...>, fn: (...any) → (), ...: T... ) → Connection Connects a function to the signal and returns the connection. Passed variadic arguments that will always be prepended to fired arguments. local signal = LemonSignal.new() local connection = signal:Connect(function(prefix: string, firedMessage: string) print(prefix, firedMessage) end, "TEST1:") signal:Fire("Hello world!") -- TEST1: Hello world!

title: DisconnectAll​
content: </> Signal.DisconnectAll(self: Signal<U...>) → () Disconnects all connections currently connected to the signal. They may be reconnected later. local signal = LemonSignal.new() local connection1 = signal:Connect(print, "TEST1:") local connection2 = signal:Connect(print, "TEST2:") signal:Fire("Hello world!") -- TEST2: Hello world! -- TEST1: Hello world! signal:DisconnectAll() signal:Fire("Goodbye World!")

title: Fire​
content: </> Signal.Fire( self: Signal<U...>, ...: U... ) → () Fires the signal and calls all connected functions with the connection's bound arguments and the fired arguments. local signal = LemonSignal.new() local connection = signal:Connect(print, "TEST1:") signal:Fire("Hello world!") -- TEST1: Hello world!

title: Wait​
content: </> Signal.Wait(self: Signal<U...>) → U... Waits until the signal is fired and returns the fired arguments. local signal = LemonSignal.new() task.delay(1, function() signal:Fire("Hello", "world!") end) local str1, str2 = signal:Wait() print(str1) -- Hello print(str2) -- world!

title: Once​
content: </> Signal.Once( self: Signal<U...>, fn: (...any) → (), ...: T... ) → () Connects a function to a signal and disconnects if after the first fire. Can be reconnected later. local signal = Signal.new() local connection = signal:Once(print, "TEST1:") signal:Fire("Hello world!") -- TEST1: Hello world! signal:Fire("Goodbye World!")
